flashcards.front: What is an encoder in digital logic?
flashcards.back: An encoder is a combinational circuit that converts 2^n input lines to n output lines, producing binary code corresponding to the active input. It performs the inverse operation of a decoder.

flashcards.front: How many output lines does a 4-to-2 encoder have?
flashcards.back: A 4-to-2 encoder has 2 output lines since 2^n = 4 means n = 2 outputs.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a binary encoder and a priority encoder?
flashcards.back: A binary encoder assumes only one input is active at a time. A priority encoder handles multiple simultaneous inputs by assigning priority to the highest-order active input.

flashcards.front: What is the output of an 8-to-3 encoder when input I5 is active?
flashcards.back: The output would be 101 (binary), which is 5 in decimal.

flashcards.front: Why can't a basic binary encoder handle multiple simultaneous inputs?
flashcards.back: Because when multiple inputs are HIGH simultaneously, the output becomes undefined or produces incorrect binary code. This is called the ambiguity problem.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the enable input in an encoder?
flashcards.back: The enable input (EN) controls when the encoder is active. When EN = 0, all outputs are disabled; when EN = 1, the encoder functions normally.

flashcards.front: How many OR gates are needed for a 4-to-2 encoder implementation?
flashcards.back: A 4-to-2 encoder requires 2 OR gates—one for each output line (Y0 and Y1).

flashcards.front: What is the truth table output for a 4-to-2 encoder when input D2 is HIGH?
What is a decimal-to-BCD encoder used for?
flashcards.back: It converts decimal digits (0-9) from 10 input keys to 4-bit BCD output code, commonly used in calculators and digital displays.

flashcards.front: What does the valid (V) output indicate in a priority encoder?
flashcards.back: V = 1 indicates at least one input is active. V = 0 indicates no inputs are active (all inputs are 0).

flashcards.front: How does a priority encoder resolve conflicts between simultaneous inputs?
flashcards.back: It uses a priority scheme—typically the highest-numbered input (or lowest, depending on design) gets priority. The output represents the highest-priority active input.
An octal-to-binary encoder converts 8 input lines (representing octal digits 0-7) to 3-bit binary output, also known as 8-to-3 encoder.

flashcards.front: What is the limitation of using only OR gates for encoder implementation?
flashcards.back: OR gate implementation only works correctly when one input is HIGH at a time. Multiple simultaneous HIGH inputs produce incorrect output.

flashcards.front: What is the inverse operation of encoding?
flashcards.back: Decoding—converting n input lines back to 2^n output lines is the inverse function performed by a decoder.

flashcards.front: How many input lines does a 16-to-4 encoder have?
flashcards.back: A 16-to-4 encoder has 16 input lines (2^4 = 16) and produces 4 output lines.
